Japanese: その子を‥‥人質をはなしなさい！
English: The girl... Let her go!

Japanese: ダマれ！　ち、近寄ると‥‥ ヒトジチ、殺すッ！
English: Shut up! C-Come closer... And I kill her!

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンだったわね。 あなたは、ここまでよ‥‥。
English: Sorry, but you're not going to get the chance...

Japanese: パァァァン！
English: *BANG!*

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくは今、ある裁判の記録を 目の前にしている。
English: I'm reading through the file of an old court case.

Japanese: ぼくの師匠、千尋さんが 初めて法廷に立ったときの記録だ。
English: It was the first case of my long-time mentor, Mia Fey...

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんが初めて弁護したのは 脱走した死刑囚だった。
English: Her very first client was a death row inmate who had recently broken out of prison.

Japanese: それは、ぼくが千尋さんに出会う さらに１年前のことだ‥‥
English: That was a whole year before Mia and I ever met.

Japanese: ～６年前～ 綾里千尋・初めての法廷
English: -- 6 Years Earlier -- Mia Fey First Trial

Japanese: ２月１６日　午前　９時２４分 地方裁判所　被告人第４控え室
English: February 16, 9:24 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 4

Japanese: （ううう‥‥もうダメ。 キンチョーで死んじゃいそう）
English: (Ugh... I'm so nervous. I feel like I'm going to die.)

Japanese: （やっぱり、やめて おけばよかったかな‥‥）
English: (I never should've accepted this case...)

Japanese: きゃああああああッ！
English: Eeeeeeek!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。お、おはようございます！ （ビビっちゃダメよ、千尋！）
English: ...Ah! G-G-Good morning! (Don't be so jumpy, Mia!)

Japanese: う。オレ。やってない！ ダレも殺してない！
English: I-I din't do nuttin'! I swear! I din't kill nobody!

Japanese: （尾並田　美散（おなみだみちる） ‥‥私の、初めての依頼人）
English: (Terry Fawles... ...My first client.)

Japanese: （５年前、死刑を宣告されて 服役中だった‥‥脱獄囚）
English: (Sentenced to death 5 years ago, and now... a prison escapee.)

Japanese: （とにかく、ここは軽い世間話ね。 リラックスさせてあげなくちゃ）
English: (Just relax, Mia! Make small talk and try to relax him!)

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あの。 どうしてダツゴクしたんですか？
English: ...Err, umm... So why did you escape anyway?

Japanese: う。う。うがー！
English: Ah. Ah. UGGA!

Japanese: きゃああ！ ゴメンなさいゴメンなさい！
English: Eeeeek! I'm sorry! I'm sorry!

Japanese: ‥‥うう。オレ、やってない！ なんにもやってない！
English: ...Ugh. I din't do nuttin'! I din't kill nobody!

Japanese: オレ。ウソ、つかない！ ダツゴクなんて、知らない！
English: I never... I never lie! I din't escape from nowhere!
Err...

Japanese: でも‥‥。現におととい、 再逮捕されたじゃないですか。
English: But, Mr. Fawles... The police just recaptured you two days ago.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥う。 ゴメン。ちょっと、ウソついた。
English: Ugh. Sorry. I told a little lie.

Japanese: （うううう‥‥）
English: (Oh boy...)

Japanese: でも！　オレ、やってない！ ダレも‥‥殺してない！
English: But anyway, I din't do it! I never killed nobody!

Japanese: あの。シツレイですけど‥‥ 死刑囚さん、なんですよね？
English: Umm, sorry for asking but... You're on death row, right?

Japanese: う。う。うがー！
English: Uh. Uh... UGGA!

Japanese: きゃああ！ ゴメンなさいゴメンなさい！
English: Eeeeeek! I'm really, really sorry!

Japanese: ‥‥死刑判決、今から５年前。 オレ、ハメられた！　ダマされた！
English: They sentenced me to die 5 years ago! But I was tricked I tell you!

Japanese: あのオンナ、ウソの証言、した。 ‥‥だから、死刑判決を‥‥！
English: That woman! She lied in her testimony! ...That's why I got the death penalty!

Japanese: オレ‥‥殺してない！ そんなコト、できるワケない。
English: I swear it! I din't kill her! I could never do that!

Japanese: （‥‥おととい、囚人護送車が 事故を起こした際、逃走‥‥）
English: (Two days ago, he escaped from the police wagon when it crashed...)

Japanese: （そして、約８時間後‥‥）
English: (Then about 8 hours later...)

Japanese: （彼が再逮捕されるまでに、 １人の婦人警官が殺害された）
English: (A policewoman was murdered before the police could recapture him.)

Japanese: （この死刑囚‥‥尾並田　美散に 殺害されたと考えられている）
English: (The police believe that Terry Fawles did it.)

Japanese: あの‥‥。脱走して、この 婦人警官と会ったんですか？
English: Umm... After you escaped, did you meet a policewoman?

Japanese: 会った。‥‥オレ、そのために 護送車、逃げ出した。
English: Yeah... I did. ...She's the reason I escaped.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥被害者と会ったのは まちがいないのね）
English: (So that much is true... He did meet with the victim.)

Japanese: でも！　オレ、殺してない！
English: But I din't kill her!

Japanese: 別れたとき‥‥あのオンナ、 生きてた！　これ、ホント！
English: She was alive when I left! She was alive...! It-It's true!

Japanese: （信じていいんですね？ 尾並田さん‥‥）
English: (I can trust him... right? I mean, I should...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
被告人をニラんだって、 真相は見えやしねえさ‥‥。
English: You're not going to figure out the truth by just staring at the guy...

Japanese: あ‥‥せ、センパイ！
English: Y-You're... Why are you here?

Japanese: 裁きの庭に放りこまれた 迷子のコネコちゃんを見に来たぜ。
English: I came to see how our little kitten was doing all alone in the big, scary lion's den.

Japanese: ‥‥いっしょに 遊んでやろうかと思ってな。
English: ...I thought maybe you'd like someone to play with.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。星影先生は‥‥？
English: Err, where is Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　おジイちゃんは、 まだベッドの中だろうぜ。
English: Ha...! That old man is probably still in bed.

Japanese: カラのボトルを抱いて、 バッカスと語らってるはずさ。
English: I bet he's clutching an empty bottle and mumbling in his sleep.

Japanese: ご不満かな？　この神乃木　荘龍 （かみのぎそうりゅう）じゃあ。
English: Aren't I good enough? After all, it's me... Diego Armando!

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: I-I didn't say...

Japanese: 星影法律事務所・ナンバーワンの センパイが、私なんかのために‥‥
English: So, Diego Armando, the finest attorney at Grossberg Law Offices, is here for me...?

Japanese: いやいや。 今朝のナンバーワンは、アンタさ。
English: No, no, no... You've got it all wrong! Today, YOU'RE the finest!

Japanese: まったく、大したクソ度胸だぜ。
English: After all, it took an amazing amount of guts to take this case!

Japanese: 初めての依頼人が‥‥ 脱獄した死刑囚なんてなァ。
English: Imagine... An escaped death row convict for a first client!

Japanese: わ‥‥私も、そう思います。 （もう、逃げ出したい気分だわ）
English: Yeah, err... Th-Thanks. (I sure wish I could get out of it though!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　安心しな。 さっき、聞いたんだが‥‥
English: Ha...! Relax. I just heard some good news.

Japanese: 今日の検事さんも‥‥ どうやら、新人らしいぜ。
English: The prosecutor for today is fresh out of his diapers as well.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか！
English: R-Really!?

Japanese: ‥‥ただし。 どこかのだれかさんとちがって、
English: However... Unlike a certain somebody who I won't mention...

Japanese: 検事局始まって以来の “天才”らしいけどな‥‥。
English: ...he's earned the reputation as a "genius" since beginning his law career.
(G-Genius...?)

Japanese: さあ、そろそろ時間だな、 コネコちゃん。
English: Well, it's about time to head in, Kitten.

Japanese: イキにやろうぜ‥‥イキによォ！
English: Sharpen those claws of yours. It's go time!

Japanese: （この世で最も孤独な場所。 それはきっと、死刑囚の独房）
English: (A solitary confinement cell for the condemned must be the world's loneliest place.)

Japanese: （‥‥私の依頼人は、 そこから必死に逃げてきた）
English: (...And that's what my client ran away from.)

Japanese: （弁護士はみんな、彼を見捨てた。 でも‥‥私にはできなかった）
English: (Every other lawyer gave up on him... But not me.)

Japanese: （彼の目からあふれ出すナミダと、 そのまっすぐなコトバ‥‥）
English: (When I saw those overflowing eyes and heard that simple, child-like voice...)

Japanese: （そこに、まぎれもない 《真実》を見た気がしたから）
English: (I just had the feeling that he was telling the truth.)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: February 16, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、尾並田　美散の法廷を しめやかに開廷いたしましょう。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Terry Fawles.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側‥‥もとより。
English: The prosecution has been ready for a while, Your Honor.

Japanese: 本日の法廷は、検察側・弁護側とも 新人と聞いているのですが‥‥？
English: I understand the lawyers for both sides are newcomers...?

Japanese: は、は、はい。綾里　千尋です。
English: Y-Y-Yes, Your Honor. I'm Mia Fey.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍（みつるぎれいじ） ‥‥と申す。
English: Miles Edgeworth... Your Honor.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥ ウワサの御剣検事くんですか。
English: So you're the new prosecutor everyone is talking about, eh?

Japanese: 最年少で検事になった、という。
English: They say you joined the prosecutor's office at quite an early age.
At 20... Your Honor.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥コネコちゃんは ウワサになってないようだな。
English: I guess our little kitten hasn't earned herself much of a reputation yet, huh?

Japanese: （負けちゃダメよ、千尋。 年下の男のコなんかに！）
English: (Come on, Mia! You can't lose! Not to someone younger than you!)

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: ...Hmph.

Japanese: 若ければイイというものではない。 私はどうかと思いますがね。
English: Young people running a trial. I'm not too sure how I feel about that.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。今回の被告人は、 現在服役中の死刑囚です。
English: ...Now then, the defendant in this case is currently a felon on death row.

Japanese: ２日前、護送車から脱走したとか。
English: Two days ago, he escaped from a police wagon. Is that correct?

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Precisely.

Japanese: この法廷で裁かれるのは、 脱獄の罪じゃねえ。
English: But the defendant is not on trial for escaping prison.

Japanese: 被告人が逃走した日に 殺害された、婦人警官‥‥
English: On the day that the defendant escaped, a policewoman was murdered...

Japanese: その死の責任が、尾並田さんに あるのか、どうか‥‥？
English: So we're here to determine if Mr. Fawles was responsible for her death...?

Japanese: そういうコトだ‥‥コネコちゃん。
English: You got it, Kitten.

Japanese: それでは、御剣検事くん。 しめやかに冒頭弁論を‥‥。
English: Well then, Mr. Edgeworth. Let's hear your opening statement.

Japanese: ‥‥今から、５年前。
English: Yes, Your Honor. It was five years ago.

Japanese: 被告人・尾並田　美散は この法廷で、死刑判決を受けた。
English: The defendant, Terry Fawles, was sentenced to death in this very court.

Japanese: 罪状は、営利誘拐および‥‥殺人。
English: His crimes were kidnapping, extortion... and murder.

Japanese: 被告人が橋の上から突きおとした 少女は、当時１４才だった。
English: The girl he threw off the bridge was only 14 years old.

Japanese: たいへん、イタましい事件ですね。 私も、よくおぼえています。
English: A truly horrible crime. I remember it well.

Japanese: 裁判は、決定的な証拠がなかった ため、ずいぶん長びいたとか。
English: There was no decisive evidence, so the trial was long and protracted.

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。ケッキョク 決め手になったのは‥‥
English: Correct. But in the end, what finally decided the case was...

Japanese: ある“目撃証言”だった。
English: ...a certain witness's testimony.

Japanese: 目撃証言‥‥
English: A witness's testimony...

Japanese: たった１人で誘拐犯と対決した 婦人警官・美柳　勇希。
English: The testimony of Detective Valerie Hawthorne; the person who confronted this criminal.

Japanese: 彼女は、その場で尾並田を逮捕。 のちに法廷でハッキリ証言した。
English: She arrested Mr. Fawles at the scene and later testified against him.

Japanese: 『尾並田は、ジャマになった人質の 少女を、川に投げ落とした』と。
English: She said she witnessed Mr. Fawles throw his young victim into the river.

Japanese: 吊り橋の下を流れる《吾童川》は、 その水量と急流で知られている。
English: For those who are not aware, Eagle River is well-known for its powerful current.

Japanese: のみこまれた死体は、ほとんど 発見されることがないという‥‥
English: Most bodies that fall in are never recovered...

Japanese: （その証言が決め手になって、 死刑判決を受けたワケね‥‥）
English: (So Ms. Hawthorne's testimony was the one that put him away...)

Japanese: もしかして‥‥その “婦人警官”というのが？
English: That policewoman you just mentioned... That wouldn't be...?

Japanese: さよう。２日前、殺害された 今回の被害者‥‥
English: Exactly. The victim. The same woman that was killed 2 days ago...

Japanese: 美柳　勇希（みやなぎゆうき） 巡査部長、というわけだ。
English: Police Sergeant... Valerie Hawthorne.

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥。
English: Aha...! I see...

Japanese: 彼女の証言のおかげで死刑判決を 受けた囚人が、２日前に脱獄した。
English: The man who was sentenced to death based on her testimony escaped two days ago...

Japanese: その目的は１つ。自分を死刑にした 婦人警官への“復讐”‥‥
English: ...with only one thing on his mind. To take revenge against the woman who convicted him.
Hmm...

Japanese: 今‥‥！　私の中で、完全に 《真相》が見えたようです。
English: Aha! The truth is becoming clear to me now!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥。
English: Yes, yes...

Japanese: これはもう、キマリでしょう。 有罪、というコトで。
English: It's quite obvious that the defendant is guilty.

Japanese: ちょちょ、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-Wait a minute! That's not right!

Japanese: ちゃんと、証言を聞いてから 決めてくださいっ！
English: At least hear the case before you decide on the outcome, Your Honor!

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥。
English: Grrrngh...

Japanese: 若さにまかせた、悪あがき。 私はどうかと思いますがね。
English: Watch yourself, Ms. Fey! I'm not sure I care for your word choice, or your tone of voice.

Japanese: ‥‥若さというのは、 ときに悲しいモノなのだ。
English: Young people these days simply don't know how to respect their elders.

Japanese: （‥‥あんた、 私より年下じゃないの！）
English: (Why you...! You're even younger than me, you hypocrite!)

Japanese: それでは、御剣検事くん。 しめやかに証人を呼んでください。
English: Now then, Mr. Edgeworth, please call your first witness.

Japanese: ‥‥初動捜査を担当した 刑事を入廷させていただこう。
English: I call the detective who was in charge of the initial investigation of this case.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。名前と職業を。
English: Witness... State your name and occupation.

Japanese: 自分の名前は糸鋸　圭介。所轄署の 殺人事件捜査担当のけ、刑事ッス！
English: Gumshoe. Dick Gumshoe. I'm the homicide detective in charge of the case, sir!

Japanese: 半年前より、ついに！　やっと！ 念願の刑事課に配属されたッス！
English: I finally got promoted to the detective division half a year ago!

Japanese: だれもそんなコト、聞いてません。
English: I don't believe anyone asked you about that.

Japanese: なんスとォ！　刑事になるのが どんなにタイヘンか‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: Hey, ma'am! You got any idea how much work it takes...

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: き‥‥キレイッス‥‥
English: You... Y-You're really gorgeous...

Japanese: え。
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: ムネのあたりが、 “キュン”となったッス。
English: No, seriously... My heart... It's aching for you...

Japanese: ‥‥刑事。シゴトをしたまえ。 さもないと‥‥
English: Detective. Pull yourself together and try to be professional. Otherwise...

Japanese: 胃のあたりが“キュン”となる 処分を下すコトになるが。
English: I'll write you up on contempt so quick that something other than your heart will ache!

Japanese: むぐ。すす、すまねッス。
English: Urk... O-OK, I-I got it!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、刑事くん。 事件の説明をおねがいします。
English: ...Now, Detective. Tell us about the incident.

Japanese: はッ！　リョーカイッス！
English: Yes, sir! Right away!

Japanese: 被害者は美柳　勇希巡査部長。 ベテランの女刑事ッス。
English: The victim was Sergeant Valerie Hawthorne, a veteran on the police force.

Japanese: 死因は、背中に刺された ナイフによる、失血死ッス。
English: She was stabbed in the back with a knife and died from excessive blood loss.

Japanese: それらの情報は、 解剖記録にも書かれていますね。
English: That much is already stated in the autopsy report.

Japanese: 事件について、もっと詳しく 聞かせていただきたい。
English: The court would like to hear more details about the incident itself.

Japanese: はッ！　リョーカイッス！ では、上面図を見ていただくッス。
English: Yes, sir! I gotcha! OK! Let's take a look at this aerial map of the area here!

Japanese: これは、現場となった吊り橋、 《おぼろ橋》の見取り図ッス。
English: This is a sketch of Dusky Bridge, an old suspension bridge.

Japanese: 橋の下を流れているのが、 かの急流《吾童川》ッス。
English: And the river that runs under there is Eagle River.

Japanese: 犯人と被害者は、 吊り橋の上で会ったッス。
English: The victim and the defendant met there... On top of the bridge.

Japanese: 被害者の背中をナイフで刺して殺害 した犯人は、死体を運び去り‥‥、
English: After stabbing her in the back, the killer carried the victim back to his car.

Japanese: クルマで逃亡したところを 検問中の警察官に逮捕されたッス。
English: He was recaptured at a police checkpoint as he was trying to make his getaway, sir.

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥なるほど。 よくわかりました。
English: Hmm... I see...

Japanese: 《おぼろ橋の上面図》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Dusky Bridge Map added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥橋の上に、被害者の 血痕でも残っていたのですかな？
English: Was the victim's blood found on the bridge?

Japanese: 被害者の美柳巡査部長は、 厚手のコートを着ていたッス。
English: The victim, Sergeant Valerie Hawthorne, was wearing a thick coat, sir.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、おぼろ橋には なんの痕跡もなかったッス。
English: Unfortunately, no traces of blood were found on the bridge.

Japanese: ぬううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 私は、“スイソク”という コトバが大キライです。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. I warn you that I absolutely despise conjecture.

Japanese: 橋に痕跡がない以上、 ２人がそこで会った証拠はない！
English: If there was no blood on the bridge, then you have no proof that they even met there!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 証言を聞いていただければ、 ナットクしていただけるだろう。
English: If you would listen to the testimony we have prepared, I'm sure you'll be convinced.

Japanese: ２人はあの日、あの橋の上で たしかに会った、と‥‥。
English: The two of them most certainly did meet on the bridge that day...

Japanese: ぬううう‥‥。
English: Why, Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: この私に、指をつきつけるタイド。 どうかと思いますがね！
English: I'm not sure I like you wagging your finger at me as though I were some hoser!

Japanese: それでは、刑事くん。 証言を命じます！
English: Detective, proceed with your testimony!

Japanese: は‥‥ハッ！
English: Um... Yes, sir!

Japanese: （いよいよ始まるわ‥‥千尋！）
English: (Here we go, Mia! Hang on...!)

Japanese: いいか‥‥よおく耳をすませ、 コネコちゃん。
English: OK now... Listen carefully, Kitten.

Japanese: 油断すると‥‥のまれちゃうぜ。 法廷の神サマに‥‥！
English: One little mistake and this guy will drink you for morning tea! Trust me and get ready.

Japanese: ～事件のあらまし～
English: -- Summary of the Incident --

Japanese: 美柳刑事は事件当日、ナニモノかに 電話で呼び出されたッス。
English: On the day of the incident, an unknown person phoned the sergeant and asked to meet.

Japanese: 彼女は指定された時間におぼろ橋へ 行き、そこで犯人に会ったッス。
English: Sergeant Hawthorne went to Dusky Bridge at the designated time and met with Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: そして‥‥彼女は、ムザンにも 殺害されてしまったッス。
English: And that's where she was brutally murdered, sir.

Japanese: 犯人は、死体をクルマのトランクに 詰めこんで、逃げようとしたッス。
English: The criminal stuffed her body into his car trunk and tried to make a getaway.

Japanese: 山のふもとに展開された検問で、 尾並田は逮捕されたッス。
English: Mr. Fawles was arrested at a police checkpoint we set up at the base of the mountain.

Japanese: ぬううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: たしかに、おぼろ橋の重要性は ハッキリしたようです。
English: Well, you certainly have established the importance of the bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥当然。
English: ...Naturally.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 しめやかに尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now, would the defense please hurry up and proceed with the cross-examination.

Japanese: は、はいッ！ じじ、じんもん‥‥ですよねっ！
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor! C-C-Cross-examination... Coming right up!

Japanese: おいおい‥‥大丈夫かい、 コネコちゃん。
English: Hey, hey! Settle down there, Kitten!

Japanese: あんまり震えると、カップに さざ波が打ち寄せちゃうぜ。
English: If you keep trembling like that, you're gonna make me spill my coffee!

Japanese: ふふ、震えてなんか、いません！ むむむむ、武者ぶるいですッ！
English: I-I-I'm not t-trembling! It-It-It's just cold in here!

Japanese: 法廷はタフな戦場だぜ。 特に、初心者さんには‥‥なァ。
English: The courtroom can be a cold battlefield alright. Especially... for a beginner.

Japanese: し、シンパイいりません！ だって‥‥
English: I-I don't need you to worry about me...! I mean...

Japanese: 犯人だって証人だって、 みーんな初心者じゃないですか！
English: I mean, the defendant, the witness... everyone's a beginner in here!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ ちげェねえぜ‥‥。
English: Ha...! You got me there.

Japanese: まずは、お手並み拝見、と行くぜ ‥‥コネコちゃん。
English: But maybe you should keep your claws out, and show them what you've got... Kitten.

Japanese: （だ、大丈夫よ、千尋！）
English: (It's OK, Mia. Stay calm...)

Japanese: （法廷の作法なら、ゆうべビデオで バッチリ、勉強したじゃない！）
English: (Just remember those court procedure videos you stayed up all last night watching!)

Japanese: ～事件のあらまし～
English: -- Summary of the Incident --

Japanese: 美柳刑事は事件当日、ナニモノかに 電話で呼び出されたッス。
English: On the day of the incident, an unknown person phoned the sergeant and asked to meet.

Japanese: 彼女は指定された時間におぼろ橋へ 行き、そこで犯人に会ったッス。
English: Sergeant Hawthorne went to Dusky Bridge at the designated time and met with Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: そして‥‥彼女は、ムザンにも 殺害されてしまったッス。
English: And that's where she was brutally murdered, sir.

Japanese: 犯人は、死体をクルマのトランクに 詰めこんで、逃げようとしたッス。
English: The criminal stuffed her body into his car trunk and tried to make a getaway.

Japanese: 山のふもとに展開された検問で、 尾並田は逮捕されたッス。
English: Mr. Fawles was arrested at a police checkpoint we set up at the base of the mountain.

Japanese: いいか、コネコちゃん。 この証言‥‥ワナが待ってるぜ。
English: Listen up, Kitten. There's a big trap waiting for you in that testimony.

Japanese: わわわ、ワナ‥‥ですか？
English: A t-trap?

Japanese: ウカツに踏みこむと、キバをむく。 ‥‥楽しいじゃねえか。
English: Walk into it carelessly, and it'll leave more than just a flesh wound. Fun, huh?

Japanese: 楽しくありません！
English: No, it's NOT!

Japanese: まあ‥‥情報を集めなきゃ 勝負にならねえ。
English: Well, if you want to have any chance at all, you'd better get some more information.

Japanese: 若いうちは、ワナに ハマっておくもんだぜ。
English: And if you're going to get caught in a trap, it's best to get caught early.

Japanese: ムジュンを探すのは‥‥ それからでも遅くねえ。
English: You can always look for contradictions afterwards.

Japanese: （ウワサに聞いてる“ムジュン”。 私に見つけられるかしら‥‥）
English: (The ever-famous "contradictions". I sure hope I can find some of those...)

Japanese: 後部トランクの写真ッス。 ヘンなトコロは見あたらないッス！
English: Here's a photo of the trunk. But I don't see anything strange, do you? Anyway...

Japanese: “ナニモノか”‥‥ということは、 正体はわかってないんですよね！
English: This "unknown person"... You have no idea who it might be, right?

Japanese: ザンネンでしたッス！
English: Sorry, but I'm afraid I do!
What...?

Japanese: 美柳刑事を呼びだしたのは、 被告人・尾並田だったッス！
English: The one who called Sergeant Hawthorne was the defendant, Terry Fawles!

Japanese: な、なんとォォッ！ 被告人が‥‥被害者に電話を！
English: Wh-Wh-Whaaat! The defendant...! The defendant called her!?

Japanese: 美柳巡査部長は、ナニゴトも キチンとするヒトだったッス。
English: Sergeant Hawthorne was a very thorough person, sir!

Japanese: 尾並田からの電話について、 キチンとメモを残していたッス。
English: She left a note about her phone call with Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: め、メモ‥‥？
English: A note...?

Japanese: 被害者のデスクに残されていた、 ゴクヒのメモッス。
English: Yeah, a top-secret memo that she left on her desk.

Japanese: 証拠品《被害者のメモ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Victim's Note added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: このメモによると、たしかに 被害者を橋に呼びだしたのは‥‥
English: According to this note, it seems the one who called her to the bridge was indeed...

Japanese: 被告人にまちがいない ようですな！
English: ...the defendant, Terry Fawles!

Japanese: うう‥‥ッ！ （目を通したほうがよさそうね）
English: Nngh! (Whose bright idea was it to keep that note from me!?)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　裁判長のヤツ、 かなりココロが動いてるぜ。
English: Ha...! Looks like the judge is even more sure of his verdict now.

Japanese: アンタ‥‥！　ヘタなツッコミは イノチ取り、だぜ‥‥。
English: Listen up! Never ask a question if you don't already know the answer!

Japanese: （あの刑事さんのせいよ！ “ナニモノか”なんて言うから）
English: (It's that detective's fault! He's the one that said, "unknown person"...!)

Japanese: あ！　あ！ そんなカオしちゃダメッス！
English: Hey now! Don't make that face at me!

Japanese: 自分は、あの若手検事に言われて、 そう証言しただけッスから！
English: I just said it that way 'cause the prosecutor told me to!

Japanese: （ワナ‥‥というワケ‥‥）
English: (Was that... a trap...?)

Japanese: （‥‥かわいいカオして、 ワリとやるわね‥‥）
English: (...With that cute face, I didn't expect him to be so sneaky.)

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 山奥の吊り橋‥‥ どうして、そんなところに？
English: A bridge up in the mountains? But why meet there?

Japanese: それは‥‥被告人にとって、 忘れられない場所だったからッス。
English: Because it is a very important place to the defendant, that's why.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: 今から５年前。‥‥被告人は 営利誘拐をくわだてたのだが‥‥
English: If you remember, 5 years ago, the defendant kidnapped a young girl.

Japanese: 吊り橋の上に追いつめられ‥‥ 人質の少女を殺害してしまう。
English: He was chased onto a bridge... And it was there that he killed his hostage.

Japanese: その現場が‥‥ 《おぼろ橋》の上だったのだ。
English: And the place where all of this occurred is, of course, Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: 橋の上で、彼に手錠をかけたのが ‥‥美柳巡査部長だったッス。
English: The very place where Sergeant Hawthorne arrested and handcuffed Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　思い出の場所で、 運命の再会、ってワケか‥‥。
English: Ha...! Returning to the scene of the crime... How nostalgic.

Japanese: 死体は、すぐ発見されたのですか？
English: Was the body of the victim discovered right away?

Japanese: そッスね。 ワレワレは素早いッスから。
English: Yeah. We were really on the ball.

Japanese: 犯行から１時間もしないうちに、 犯人を追いつめたッス。
English: We found the criminal within one hour of the murder.

Japanese: 『動くな！　ワレワレは完全に包囲 されているッ！』‥‥みたいな。
English: It was great! We even got to say, "Don't move! We've got you surrounded!"

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、 おかしくない？　千尋‥‥）
English: (...Wait a second. Isn't there something weird about that...?)

Japanese: ‥‥現場は、山奥の吊り橋だ。
English: The location was a suspension bridge, up in the mountains.

Japanese: “なぜ、そんなに早く 事件が発覚したのか”‥‥？
English: So how did they find out about the crime so quickly...?

Japanese: 美柳巡査部長が、電話の内容を 報告していたから、でしょうか？
English: Sergeant Hawthorne must have mentioned the phone call to someone else, right?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
もし、そうしていたら 彼女は殺されなかっただろうぜ。
English: If that's what had happened, then she wouldn't have been killed.

Japanese: 尾並田からの電話は、報告されて いなかったッス。ただ‥‥
English: She never mentioned the phone call from Mr. Fawles. But...

Japanese: 机の上に、被害者の メモが残っていたッス。
English: She left a note on her desk about it.

Japanese: もう少し早く気づいていれば‥‥ 彼女は死なずにすんだッス。
English: If only I had noticed it earlier... Maybe she'd still be alive.

Japanese: （なぜ‥‥被害者は、電話のことを 報告しなかったのかしら‥‥）
English: (I wonder why she didn't mention the phone call to anyone...?)

Japanese: 尾並田さん‥‥クルマを 用意してたんですか？
English: Mr. Fawles had a car, then?

Japanese: 現場の吊り橋は、 山奥にあるッスから‥‥
English: Well, that bridge is way up in the mountains, ma'am.

Japanese: ２人とも、現場まで クルマを使っているッス。
English: The defendant and the victim both went up there by car. I mean, how else, right?

Japanese: なんと！　被告人は、 自動車を持っていたのですか？
English: What! You mean the defendant drove his own car?

Japanese: ‥‥いやいや。 モチロン、盗難車ッス。
English: No, no, of course not. ...It was stolen.

Japanese: 信号待ちをしていた若いカップルの クルマを、取りあげたッス。
English: He stole it from a young couple that had been waiting at a red light.

Japanese: ヒトのクルマを盗むとは‥‥ 私はどうかと思いますね。
English: Hmm... Car thieves... I'm not sure how I feel about car thieves.

Japanese: （他に“どうかと思う” 部分はないのかしら‥‥）
English: (Is this guy sure about how he feels about ANYTHING?)

Japanese: これが、その盗難車の 後部トランクの写真ッス。
English: This is a photo of the stolen car's trunk.

Japanese: ‥‥写っているのは、 モチロン美柳　勇希ッス。
English: ...Naturally, that's the body of Valerie Hawthorne in there.

Japanese: うわ！　これは‥‥ 死んでますねえ‥‥。
English: Whoa! That... That doesn't look too comfortable...

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 被害者は‥‥たしか、 背中を刺されたんですよね？
English: The victim... She was stabbed in the back, correct?

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥オトコは常に、 背中を狙われているもんだぜ‥‥
English: ...For some reason, men always seem to get stabbed in the back...

Japanese: （亡くなったの、 女性なんだけどな‥‥）
English: (We're talking about a woman here...)

Japanese: この写真では見えないッスが‥‥
English: You can't tell from this photo, but...

Japanese: 被害者の背中には、ちゃーんと ナイフが刺さっていたッス。
English: ...the knife was stuck in her back nice and firm.

Japanese: ‥‥死体が発見された状況は とても重要です。
English: The condition of the body when it was discovered is very important information.

Japanese: 刑事くん。現場に、何か おかしな点はなかったのですか？
English: Detective, was there anything strange or noteworthy in the trunk of the car?

Japanese: ずいぶん、手回しが よかったんですね。
English: That certainly is some impressive police work.

Japanese: いやー、あぶない ところだったッス。
English: Well, no, actually it was way too close for comfort.

Japanese: 検問が設置されたのが、 午後５時すぎッスから‥‥
English: We set up that checkpoint just after 5:00 PM...

Japanese: 尾並田を取り逃がしても フシギじゃなかったッス。
English: We figured that Mr. Fawles might just try to run.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか‥‥？
English: ...What do you mean it was too close for comfort?

Japanese: ２人が会うヤクソクをした時刻は 午後４時３０分だった。
English: The two of them arranged to meet at 4:30 PM.

Japanese: 検問のポイントから橋までは、 クルマで約３０分‥‥だ。
English: And it takes approximately 30 minutes to go from the bridge to the checkpoint.

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、 ギリギリだったワケね‥‥）
English: (Hmm... That WAS kinda close. Any later and Mr. Fawles could have slipped right by.)

Japanese: この写真について、被告人は どう言ってるんですか？
English: What did the defendant have to say about this photo?

Japanese: もー、話にならないッス。
English: What he always says, ma'am!

Japanese: オレ、知らねえ！ 何もやってねえ！
English: I din't do it! I din't do nuttin'!!

Japanese: ‥‥こればっかりッス。
English: ...That's all he says.

Japanese: “何も”って‥‥。現に自動車を 盗んだわけでしょうが！
English: "Nuttin'"? I wouldn't say he did "nuttin'". At the very least, we know he stole a car!

Japanese: 自分もそう言ってやったッス。 そうしたら、
English: It's just what he always says, Your Honor. And then he always says...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥う。 ゴメン。ちょっと、ウソついた。
English: Uh. Sorry. I told a little lie.

Japanese: ‥‥だそうッス。
English: ...Or something like that.

Japanese: とにかく、ほとんど現行犯 逮捕だったわけですね。
English: Well, in any case, it seems he was caught and arrested.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Precisely.

Japanese: ‥‥証人っ！
English: ...Witness!

Japanese: な、なんですか！　この、 いたたまれないキンチョー感は！
English: Wh-What is it...? Do you have something to say, Ms. Fey!?

Japanese: す、すみません！　アタマの中、 まっ白になっちゃって。
English: I-I'm sorry! I-I totally forgot what I was going to say...

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトさまに指をつきつけるの、 初めてなものですから‥‥
English: This is... This is the first time I've ever had to actually address someone like that...

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。 ちゃんと、練習しておきなさいッ！
English: Grrgh... You should have practiced before coming to court!

Japanese: まったく‥‥ 私はどうかと思いますね！
English: Honestly, Ms. Fey... I'm not sure I like this!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　コネコちゃん。 コーヒーキャンデー、なめるかい？
English: Hmph...! Say there, little Kitten, want a piece of my coffee candy?

Japanese: キャンデー？
English: Candy?

Japanese: ‥‥ホンモノのコーヒーは、 アンタには早すぎるからな。
English: Well, you're still too young to be drinking real coffee.

Japanese: （くぅぅ‥‥このクヤシサを たたきつけてやるの、千尋！）
English: (Grrr... Come on, Mia! Shake it off! You're a lawyer!)

Japanese: ‥‥こらっ、刑事さんっ！
English: Detective!

Japanese: は、はいッスッ！
English: Y-Yes, ma'am!

Japanese: この写真‥‥
English: This photo...

Japanese: “ヘンなトコロはない” ‥‥そう言いましたね？
English: You said that there was nothing peculiar about it. ...Is that correct?

Japanese: い、言っちまったッスッ！
English: Y-Yeah, that's what I said!

Japanese: しかし‥‥被害者が残した、 このメモをよく読みなさいっ！
English: Well then, I suggest you take another look at the note written by the victim!

Japanese: め、めもめも‥‥
English: The n-n-note...?

Japanese: はっきり、こう書いてあります。 《目印に、白のマフラー用意》‥‥
English: It very clearly says, "Wear white scarf for identification."...

Japanese: おそらく、被害者の カオを忘れていたのでしょう。
English: The caller must have forgotten what the victim looked like. Thus, this special request.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああッ‥‥！
English: Aaah... I, umm...!

Japanese: そのマフラー‥‥ いったい、どこに消えたのかしら？
English: I have one very simple question for you, Detective. Where is the white scarf?

Japanese: ‥‥この写真には 写っていないようですけど‥‥
English: I can't seem to find it in this photo...

Japanese: そのぉ‥‥トランクからは 見つからなかったッス。
English: Um... Well, to be honest, we didn't find it in the trunk, ma'am...

Japanese: ダメじゃないの！ ちゃんと探さなきゃ！
English: And you stopped there!? You should have looked for it!

Japanese: むぐわああああ‥‥ッス！
English: Aaaaaaarrrggg...!

Japanese: 犯人から指定された以上‥‥ 彼女はマフラーをつけていたはず！
English: The caller told her to wear it to identify herself, so I'd expect she did just that!

Japanese: ‥‥そのあたり、いかがですかッ！ 御剣検事くんッ！
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth!? What do you have to say about this!?

Japanese: ふぅ‥‥やれやれ。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: 弁護側には、よほど 材料がないと見える‥‥
English: I see the defense is a little... lacking.

Japanese: お探しのマフラーというのは‥‥ これのこと、だろうか。
English: The scarf you are searching so desperately for... ...Is it this one, perchance?

Japanese: あ‥‥っ！
ど、どこにあったッス！
English: Wh-Where did you find that, sir!?

Japanese: ‥‥現場の吊り橋の上だ。 私が、まっ先に調べた。
English: On Dusky Bridge. I was there first, and decided to conduct my own investigation.

Japanese: な‥‥なんで自分にも 教えてくれなかったッスか！
English: Wh-Why...? Why didn't you tell me?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠品は、自分のカバンに しまうことにしている。
English: I made a decision to keep all pieces of evidence in my personal satchel.

Japanese: 私の知るかぎり、 イチバン安全な場所だ。
English: It's the safest place I know.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　あのボウヤ‥‥、 ‥‥キチンと、わかってやがるぜ。
English: Hmph! That hot-shot sure has a flair for the dramatic...

Japanese: 被害者のメモとちがって、 《白》のマフラーではないが‥‥
English: It's not exactly "white", as the caller requested...

Japanese: おそらく、近い色で 代用したのだろう。
English: But as you can see, it's close enough, for what it was intended for.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。 かなりドロでヨゴれています。
English: Hmmm... It looks like it spent some time in the mud.

Japanese: ムリもない。‥‥事件当時、 吾童山中は小雨がふっていた。
English: Not surprising... It was drizzling on the mountain that day.

Japanese: （あの検事さん‥‥ワザと今まで かくしていたのね‥‥！）
English: (Prosecutor Edgeworth... He was intentionally hiding that scarf the whole time!)

Japanese: そのマフラー、証拠として しめやかに受理しましょう。
English: The court will accept the scarf into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《マフラー》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Scarf added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人が 大きくスベったところで、
English: Now, if the attorney for the defense is finished embarrassing herself...

Japanese: 次の証言にうつりたい。 よろしいかな？　弁護士クン。
English: ...I'd like to move on with the testimony. That IS alright with you, isn't it, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: （このマフラー、ギュギュウと 巻きつけてやりたい‥‥）
English: (Boy would I like to wrap this scarf around his smarmy little neck...)

Japanese: その、ドロまみれのマフラーを ふり回すまでもなく‥‥
English: Very good. Now, if we're done with this mud-covered scarf business...

Japanese: 尾並田　美散と美柳　勇希は、 おぼろ橋でジッサイに会っている。
English: ...the prosecution moves to establish conclusively, and with hard evidence that...

Japanese: ‥‥そして、橋の上で いったい、何があったのか‥‥？
English: ...Ms. Hawthorne and Mr. Fawles did indeed meet on that bridge that day.

Japanese: それを、カンペキに 立証してごらんに入れたい。
English: Further, we will show exactly what occurred there.

Japanese: それはおもしろい。 やってもらいましょうか。
English: That sounds quite promising. I can't wait to hear all about it.

Japanese: （‥‥完全に アイツのペースね‥‥）
English: (Argh... Everything is moving at his whim...)

Japanese: 忘れるな。あのボウヤが なんと呼ばれているか。
English: Don't forget, Kitten. There's a reason why everyone considers this kid a genius.
(A genius, huh...)

Japanese: ～おぼろ橋で何があったか？～
English: -- Events on Dusky Bridge --

Japanese: じつは、この事件には れっきとした目撃者がいたッス。
English: Actually, there's an eyewitness who was there when the incident took place.

Japanese: 目撃者がグーゼン撮った写真ッス。 きっちりマフラーを巻いてるッス。
English: This photo was accidentally taken by the witness. It shows her wearing the scarf, sir.

Japanese: 事件当時は小雨がパラついてたので ちょっと、ボヤけてるッスけど。
English: It was drizzling that day; unfortunately, it's a little hard to see what's going on.

Japanese: 犯人は、押し倒した被害者の背中に またがって、ナイフを刺したッス！
English: Anyway, the criminal shoved the victim down from behind and stabbed her in the back!

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、マフラーはそのとき はずれたと考えられるッス。
English: ...That must have been when the scarf fell off.

Japanese: ぬううううう。 この写真の感じでは‥‥
English: Hmm. Looking at this photo...

Japanese: 橋は、かなり高いところに かかっているようですね。
English: ...you really get the sense that this bridge is very high up.

Japanese: 下を流れる吾童川から、 約１２メートルの高さ‥‥だ。
English: It is about a 40 feet drop from the bridge to the Eagle River down below.

Japanese: それで、御剣くん！　この写真は いったい、どなたが？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, who took this photo anyway?

Japanese: 善意の第三者‥‥ といったところだろうか。
English: Let's just say that it was a well-intentioned third party.

Japanese: つまり《目撃者》でしょう！ なぜ、出廷していないのですか？
English: Aha! A potential witness! So why isn't this person in the courtroom?

Japanese: それが、その‥‥どうしても 証言台には立ちたくない、と。
English: Well... They said they absolutely did not want to testify.

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、非常にセンサイな ココロの持ち主なのだ。
English: ...The person in question is very delicate, Your Honor.

Japanese: この写真さえあれば、 証言の必要はない。
English: Besides, as long as we have this photo, we see no reason to compel her to testify.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。 私はどうかと思いますがね！
English: I'm not sure how I feel about that!

Japanese: 証拠品《目撃写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Witness's Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 尾並田　美散には動機があり、 こうして、現場で会っている‥‥
English: So, as you can see, Terry Fawles had both the motive and the opportunity.

Japanese: そのイミするところは、 アキラカだと思われるが‥‥？
English: I think it's quite clear at this point what happened on that bridge.
Hmm.

Japanese: 今‥‥！　私の中で、完全に 《真相》が見えたようです。
English: Aha! The truth is becoming clear to me now!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 今度こそ、キマリでしょう！ 有罪、というコトで。
English: Yes, it's quite obvious. He's clearly guilty.

Japanese: だ、だから、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: N-Not again! That's not fair!

Japanese: がんばって尋問しますから、 ちゃんと聞いてください！
English: I haven't even done my cross-examination yet!

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （“ぬうううう”じゃないわよ！）
English: (What do you mean, "Hmm"!?)

Japanese: ～おぼろ橋で何があったか？～
English: -- Events on Dusky Bridge --

Japanese: じつは、この事件には れっきとした目撃者がいたッス。
English: Actually, there's an eyewitness who was there when the incident took place.

Japanese: 目撃者がグーゼン撮った写真ッス。 きっちりマフラーを巻いてるッス。
English: This photo was accidentally taken by the witness. It shows her wearing the scarf, sir.

Japanese: 事件当時は小雨がパラついてたので ちょっと、ボヤけてるッスけど。
English: It was drizzling that day; unfortunately, it's a little hard to see what's going on.

Japanese: 犯人は、押し倒した被害者の背中に またがって、ナイフを刺したッス！
English: Anyway, the criminal shoved the victim down from behind and stabbed her in the back!

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、マフラーはそのとき はずれたと考えられるッス。
English: ...That must have been when the scarf fell off.

Japanese: ‥‥おそれいったぜ。 あんな写真まであったとはなァ。
English: Heh, talk about a surprise. I had no idea there was a photo.

Japanese: わ‥‥私、どうすれば‥‥！
English: S-So what do I do... ?

Japanese: これでも‥‥まだ、 信じるってのか？　あの死刑囚‥‥
English: You really still believe him? Mr. Crybaby, I mean?

Japanese: も、もちろんです！
English: Of course I do!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　コネコちゃんは メルヘンがお好き、か‥‥。
English: Hmph...! So the little kitten believes in fairy tales, huh...

Japanese: それなら、コタエはカンタンだ。
English: In that case, the answer is obvious.

Japanese: アンタの信じていることが 《真実》ならば‥‥
English: If what you believe is the truth...

Japanese: この証言の中に、かならず かくれているハズだろうぜ。
English: ...then that means that somewhere, hidden in that testimony, is a contradiction.

Japanese: でっかいムジュンが、１ケ。 ‥‥それだけのコトさ。
English: One huge contradiction waiting to be discovered. ...That's your chance.

Japanese: その《目撃者》というのは‥‥？
English: Who is this eyewitness?

Japanese: 女子大生ッス！
English: She's a college student!

Japanese: ‥‥女子大生‥‥？
English: A female college student...?

Japanese: そッス！ 女子で、かつ大学生ッス！
English: That's right! Meaning she's "female" AND a "college student", ma'am!

Japanese: 彼女、人前に出るのがニガテなので かわりに自分が来ちゃったッス。
English: She doesn't do well in front of other people, so I came to testify for her.

Japanese: しかし！　弁護側には、 目撃証人を尋問するケンリが‥‥
English: Maybe so! But as the attorney for the defense, I have the right to cross-examine her...

Japanese: 今の時点で、証人のコトバは 必要なし。‥‥それだけだ。
English: For the time being, we're not relying on the witness's statements... That is all.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですかっ！
English: Wh-What is that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: ‥‥こちらには、 決定的な証拠品がある。
English: ...The prosecution has other, more decisive evidence.

Japanese: 女子大生のアイマイな証言など、 まったく不要！
English: Our case doesn't rest on the vague testimony of a female college student.

Japanese: 女子大生、ですか‥‥
English: A female college student, eh.

Japanese: 女子で、かつ大学生ッス！
English: It means she's "female" AND a "college student", sir!

Japanese: 彼女の証言が聞きたければ‥‥ その必要性を作り出すことだ。
English: If you absolutely must hear her testimony, you'll have to give us a good reason why.

Japanese: くぅぅ‥‥。
English: Grrr...

Japanese: それで、その“決定的な証拠” というのが‥‥？
English: Please tell us about the more decisive evidence in question.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥被害者は マフラーをしているわね‥‥）
English: (The victim is wearing a scarf in that photo alright.)

Japanese: ところで‥‥この写真を 撮った《目撃者》ですけど‥‥
English: So, about the witness who took this photo...

Japanese: 何をしていたのかしら？ ‥‥こんな、山の中で。
English: What was this person doing all the way in the mountains?

Japanese: なんでも、野草の 撮影をしていたらしいッス。
English: She was taking photos of wild flowers apparently.

Japanese: あの山には、野生の薬草が多い。
English: There are many unusual types of flora on that mountain, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: 周辺に住む人々によれば、 吾童山に宿る霊気のせいだ、とか。
English: People in the area say it's because of the spirits that live there.

Japanese: れれれ、霊気！ そういえば、この写真‥‥
English: S-S-Spirits! Now that you mention it, th-this photo...

Japanese: ボンヤリ、けむって見えるのは‥‥ オバケですか！　私は信じません！
English: This cloudy fog-like thing... Is-Is it a ghost!? I-I don't believe it!

Japanese: いやいや。それはたぶん‥‥ オバケじゃないと思うッス。
English: No, Your Honor, no... I don't think it's a ghost.

Japanese: 小雨‥‥ですか。
English: Drizzling, huh?

Japanese: そッス。細かい雨がシトシトと、 かなりシメっぽかったッス。
English: That's right. There was a light rain coming down. The whole place was dreary...

Japanese: ‥‥ま。この法廷のムード ほどじゃなかったッスけど。
English: ...But not as dreary as the mood that's in this court room right now. Ha ha.

Japanese: ‥‥さらに シメっぽくなっちまったッス。
English: ...Looks like a cold front just moved in.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥。
English: In any case...

Japanese: 現場付近は少し、ぬかるんでいた。 キリも出ていたようだ。
English: The point is that the area was quite damp. There was even some fog.

Japanese: 自分も橋の上でシリモチついて、 エラいコトになったッス。
English: I even slipped and fell while I was on the bridge. It was really something.

Japanese: それも、《目撃者》の証言ですか？
English: Is that part of the witness's testimony as well?

Japanese: もちろん、そッス。
English: Of course it is.

Japanese: 犯人に背中を“ドン”と押されて、 そのまま、うつぶせに倒れたッス！
English: He pushed the victim hard in the back and she fell down right on her stomach!

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 私にも、おぼえがありますね。 そういうコト。
English: ...I remember that happening once myself. It was really broodle.

Japanese: （そういう場面を見たのか、 だれかに背中を押されたのか？）
English: (Are you talking about seeing someone get pushed, or were you the one getting pushed?)

Japanese: （それとも、いっそだれかの 背中を押したのか‥‥？）
English: (Or does it mean that you pushed someone down like that once...?)

Japanese: （ナゾの多い裁判長ね‥‥）
English: (With his mind-boggling tales and the way he said, "brutal", I wonder if he's Canadian...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
アンタ、ニラメッコの相手を まちがえてるぜ。
English: Save your nasty look for the right person.
Huh...?

Japanese: ‥‥見ろよ。
English: ...Take a look.

Japanese: 法廷記録が、さみしがって‥‥ ぬれちゃってるぜ。
English: Poor baby... The Court Record seems to have wet itself.

Japanese: きゃあ！　こ、コーヒーを こぼさないでください！
English: Hey! Watch where you spill your coffee!

Japanese: （法廷記録‥‥か‥‥）
English: (The Court Record, huh...)

Japanese: つまり‥‥犯人と被害者は もみ合ったわけですか？
English: So in other words, there was a struggle between the criminal and the victim, huh?

Japanese: 目撃者も、そう言ってたッス。
English: That's what the witness said.

Japanese: ま。マフラーのコトは おぼえてなかったみたいッスけど。
English: Well. It looks like she didn't remember about the scarf.

Japanese: かなりハゲしく争ったと 考えられるッス。
English: But from what she said, it sounded like a pretty violent fight, ma'am.

Japanese: （現場は、小雨でぬかるんでいた。 たぶん、橋の上も‥‥）
English: (The area was wet from rain. The bridge was probably wet, too.)

Japanese: （だから、マフラーにドロが ついているワケだけど‥‥）
English: (Which would explain why the scarf was all covered in mud, but...)

Japanese: （どこか‥‥何かが ココロに引っかかるわ‥‥）
English: (There's something about this testimony that's still bothering me...)

Japanese: 事件当時‥‥現場には、 小雨がふっていたそうですね。
English: So at the time of the crime, there was a light drizzle coming down, correct?

Japanese: キリも出て、しっとりした ムードだったッス。
English: Yeah, and fog, too. Just a generally soggy atmosphere.

Japanese: ‥‥その、しっとりしたムードに そぐわない証拠があります。
English: Well, I have evidence that doesn't go with the soggy atmosphere.

Japanese: これは‥‥トランクから発見された 被害者の死体、ですな。
English: But this is a photo of the victim's body that was found in the car trunk.

Japanese: 現場の状況から考えて、この 写真には不自然な点があります。
English: Considering the conditions at the scene of the crime, something isn't right.

Japanese: 不自然な点‥‥おもしろい。 ぜひ、うかがいたいものです。
English: Well by all means... Please enlighten us as to what isn't right!

Japanese: このトランクの写真のどこが‥‥ 事件当日の状況とムジュンするか！
English: What is it about this photo of the trunk that doesn't fit with the conditions that day?

Japanese: それは、モチロン‥‥ このへんです！　‥‥たぶん。
English: Naturally, the answer is... right around HERE! ...I think.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、御剣検事くん！
English: ...Well, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人にも トランクが必要らしい。
English: It seems to me that Ms. Fey could use a trunk herself.

Japanese: え。
English: What...?

Japanese: ニガい思い出を詰めこんで、 この街を旅立つためのトランクが。
English: One that she could hide all her bitter memories from this trial in, that is.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥さすが、天才と 言われるだけのコトはある。
English: Ho ho ho ho ho... You truly are a genius...

Japanese: なかなか シャレたことを言いますね。
English: A genius of sarcasm and word-play.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ ゼンゼンちがったみたいね）
English: (Oops! It looks like I really put my foot in my mouth that time!)

Japanese: 安心するんだ‥‥コネコちゃん。
English: Just relax. Lick your wounds clean and then go after him again, Kitten.

Japanese: どんな思い出も‥‥コーヒーの ニガさにゃ、かなわねえだろうぜ。
English: No matter how bitter the memory... It can never be as bitter as dark black coffee.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、もう一度 考えなおすの、千尋！）
English: (Alright, Mia! You have got to think harder this time!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、もう一度 うかがいましょう‥‥。
English: Now then, let me ask you one more time...

Japanese: ムジュンするところ‥‥ モチロン、ここです！
English: Naturally the answer is... right here!

Japanese: 被害者のコート‥‥ですか。
English: The victim's... coat?

Japanese: 私の見たところ、 異常はないようですが‥‥
English: As far as I can see, there is nothing strange about it.

Japanese: それがすでに、異常なのです。
English: That's exactly what's strange!

Japanese: 思い出してください！ ‥‥事件当日の、橋の上の状況を。
English: Remember the testimony! What were the conditions on the bridge that day?

Japanese: 小雨とキリで、おぼろ橋は、 ぬれていたそうです。
English: It was drizzling and foggy. Dusky Bridge was all wet.

Japanese: もし、被害者が橋の上に うつぶせに倒されたのならば‥‥
English: If the victim really had fallen down on her stomach on top of the bridge...

Japanese: 被害者のコートは、ドロで 汚れていなければおかしいのです！
English: ...then the front of her coat should have been covered in mud!
Urk...!

Japanese: それは‥‥そのとおりです！
English: That... That's exactly right!

Japanese: 私も先日、ドロ道で転んだとき ジマンのヒゲがものすごいコトに！
English: The other day I fell on a muddy street and my gorgeous playoff beard was befouled!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥ 現場はかなり、ぬかるんでいた。
English: ...I do admit that the crime scene was quite wet that day.

Japanese: しかし！　橋の上が ドロで汚れていたとはかぎらない！
English: However! That doesn't mean that the top of the bridge itself was muddy!

Japanese: 裁判長がドロ道ではなく、 フロ場で転んだとしたら‥‥
English: If Your Honor had fallen in the shower instead of on a muddy street...

Japanese: そのジマンのヒゲは、 ぬれるだけですんだはず！
English: ...his glorious hockey beard, pride of the Legal League, would be wet, but not muddy!

Japanese: さすがに、そんなに転びません。 しかし、その主張は認めます。
English: Fortunately, I have yet to test that. Still, your point is well-taken.

Japanese: 事件当日の橋の上の状況‥‥ キミに立証できると言うのかな？
English: Can you prove that the surface of the bridge was muddy that day?

Japanese: （橋の上の状況‥‥か）
English: (The surface of the bridge, huh...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ここで引いたら オトコじゃねえぜ。
English: Ha...! A real man wouldn't stand for a taunt like this!

Japanese: （ここで引いたら‥‥ オンナでもないわ！）
English: (Neither would a real woman!)

Japanese: モチロン、立証できます。
English: Of course I can!

Japanese: おぼろ橋の上はドロで汚れていた。 その証拠は‥‥！
English: Here is the evidence that proves the surface of the bridge was muddy!

Japanese: その証拠は‥‥っ！
English: The evidence is...

Japanese: 待ちな。
English: Wait a minute.

Japanese: な、なんですか！ 今、いいところなんですから‥‥
English: Wh-What is it? Your timing is terrible...!

Japanese: その証拠を提出する‥‥ そいつは、まるで‥‥そうだな。
English: If you present that evidence, it would be like...

Japanese: １００階の屋上から、煮えたぎった マグマに飛びこむようなもんだぜ。
English: Like jumping off a 100-story building into a bubbling pool of magma.

Japanese: ‥‥クビに１０トンの岩を くくりつけて、な。
English: ...After tying a 10-ton boulder around your neck.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What is THAT supposed to mean?

Japanese: ヒトコトで言えば‥‥、 自殺行為、ってコトだ。
English: To put it simply... It would be suicide.

Japanese: （最初からヒトコトで 言ってほしかったな‥‥）
English: (Why didn't he just put it simply in the first place...?)

Japanese: ‥‥人生相談は 終わったかな？　弁護人。
English: Is your little chat finished? Can we move on now?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。 思いとどまるコトにしました。
English: Y-Yes... I-I've changed my mind.

Japanese: 人生は長くありません。 早めに考えなおしなさい。
English: Life is short, Ms. Fey. Hurry it up already!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく！　弁護側には 立証の用意があります！
English: Yes, Your Honor! The defense is now prepared to show our evidence!

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥このマフラーです！
English: The evidence is... this scarf!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
‥‥説明は不要でしょう。
English: It should be obvious...

Japanese: 橋の上に落ちていたマフラーが こんなに汚れている以上‥‥
English: If the scarf fell onto the bridge and got this muddy...

Japanese: ‥‥《おぼろ橋》は事件当時、 ドロでぬかるんでいたのです！
English: It means that the bridge was obviously covered in mud!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ムムゥ‥‥ッ！ ‥‥新米弁護士があああ‥‥
English: Urrrnngh...! No... I can't be outwitted by this novice bimbo...!

Japanese: （それは、おたがいさまでしょ！）
English: (Hey! Same to you, buddy!)

Japanese: どうやら、弁護人の主張は スジが通っているようです‥‥
English: Ms. Fey's assertion makes perfect sense to me...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥このマフラーと 被害者のコートの状態には‥‥
English: I do admit that there appears to be a contradiction between...

Japanese: ムジュンが存在するようだ。
English: ...the condition of the victim's coat and her scarf.

Japanese: しかし！　重要なのは、 “それが何をイミするか”‥‥だ。
English: However! The real question is... why is there a contradiction?
Huh?

Japanese: ムジュンには‥‥かならず、 《理由》が存在する。
English: For every contradiction, there exists an explanation.

Japanese: その《理由》を、うかがおうッ！
English: Let's look at what the explanation in this case may be, shall we?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥！ （そんなの、私に聞かれても‥‥）
English: A-Alright...! (It's not like he's really giving me a choice here...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
いいセン、行ってるじゃねえか。 ‥‥コネコちゃんにしては。
English: You're doing pretty good. ...For a little kitten.

Japanese: か、神乃木さん！
English: M-Mr. Armando!

Japanese: いつの時代も‥‥ムジュンは ウソから生まれるんだ。
English: No matter what he says, a contradiction always comes down to a lie.

Japanese: トランクから発見された 《被害者》‥‥
English: It's either the victim discovered in the trunk...

Japanese: 目撃写真に写っている 《被害者と被告人》‥‥
English: ...the witness's photo showing the defendant and the victim...

Japanese: そして、犯行の瞬間を見たという 《目撃者の証言》‥‥。
English: ...or the witness's testimony that stated she saw the moment of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥落ちついて考えるんだ。 シンプルなハナシだろう？
English: Just relax and think it over. It's pretty simple, isn't it?

Japanese: これらの３つのうち、どれかが‥‥ 《ニセの手がかり》なのさ！
English: The false evidence... It's one of those three.

Japanese: べ、弁護人！　今の発言、 私はどうかと思いますな！
English: Hmph! What you said just now! I'm not sure I like that!

Japanese: あ！　今のは、私じゃなくて、 このコーヒーのヒトが勝手に‥‥
English: Th-That wasn't me, Your Honor! It was the coffee aficionado over here that said it!

Japanese: この私の法廷で《ニセの手がかり》 など、認めません！
English: This court is not in the habit of accepting false evidence, you know!

Japanese: あのボウヤ‥‥その《手がかり》を かくそうとしていやがるのさ。
English: Blame it on him, Your Honor. He's the one trying to slip false evidence into the court.

Japanese: そうはさせねえ。 引きずり出してやるんだ！
English: But we won't let him! We'll expose his evidence as the flimsy scam it really is!

Japanese: は、はいっ！
English: Y-Yes!

Japanese: このムジュンにおける、 《ニセの手がかり》‥‥それは！
English: The false evidence in this case is the...

Japanese: 被害者が犯人ともみ合い、 橋の上に倒されたのなら‥‥
English: If the victim really did try to repel her killer and if she did fall down on the bridge...

Japanese: そのコートは、汚れていなければ ならないのです。
English: ...then you would expect her coat to be dirty.

Japanese: すなわち！　このトランクから 発見された死体は‥‥
English: Therefore! The body that was found in the trunk of the car...

Japanese: 美柳　勇希さん‥‥ 被害者ではなかったのです！
English: It was NOT the body of Valerie Hawthorne!

Japanese: い‥‥いかがですか、 御剣検事くん！
English: Wh-What do you have to say to that, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ふぅ‥‥異議あり。
English: *sigh* Objection.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥。 タメ息まじり、かつ小声ですか。
English: Hmm... Not only a whisper, but he mixed in a sigh, too.

Japanese: 美柳　勇希は警察の人間‥‥ しかも、巡査部長だ。
English: Valerie Hawthorne was more than a simple meter maid; she was a sergeant.

Japanese: その身元のカクニンを、 まちがえるワケがないではないか。
English: There's absolutely no chance that a mistake about her identity could be made.

Japanese: うううう‥‥ （そりゃ、そうよね‥‥）
English: Uuurgh... (I guess he's right...)

Japanese: 証拠なき告発には、これより ペナルティを与えますぞ！
English: From this point on, Ms. Fey, I will penalize you for making unsubstantiated accusations!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥泣くのは ウチに帰ってからよ、千尋！）
English: (Ugggh... Don't do it, Mia! Don't cry until you get home!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥！
English: In any case...!

Japanese: 《目撃者》が撮ったという この写真ですが‥‥
English: About the photo that the prosecution claims was taken by a witness...

Japanese: たしかに、囚人服のオトコと、 コートを着た女性が写っています。
English: It certainly seems to show a man in a prison uniform and a woman in a coat.

Japanese: しかし‥‥！　この写真だけでは、 それ以上のことはわかりません！
English: However! We can't tell any more than that from the photo!

Japanese: 写真の人物が‥‥ 被告人と被害者ではない、とでも？
English: Are you saying the people in the photo may not be the victim and the defendant?

Japanese: そ、その可能性は じゅうぶん、あります！
English: W-Well, that's certainly a possibility!

Japanese: “囚人服”と“婦人警察官”‥‥
English: So perhaps it was another prisoner and policewoman?

Japanese: 変わりダネのカップルがもう一組、 おぼろ橋でデートしていた、と？
English: I admit, people have their quirks. Perhaps they were part of a role-playing group?

Japanese: そ、その可能性は‥‥ 少し、あるかも‥‥
English: W-Well, I suppose that's also p-possible... I guess...

Japanese: みっともねえぜ‥‥コネコちゃん。
English: You messed up again... Kitten.

Japanese: か‥‥神乃木さん！
English: M-Mr. Armando!

Japanese: もっと‥‥アキラカに アヤシイ手がかりがあるはずだ。
English: There should be something else that's even more suspicious.

Japanese: もう一度、考えてみるんだな。
English: Now think the whole thing over again.

Japanese: 考えるまでもなく‥‥ アヤシイのは《目撃者》です！
English: It is a no-brainer. Obviously it's the witness that's suspicious!

Japanese: 刑事さんは、さっき‥‥ こう証言しましたね。
English: During his earlier testimony, the detective pointed out a crucial fact.

Japanese: 『犯人は、背中から被害者を 押し倒して、ナイフを刺した』
English: "The criminal shoved the victim down from behind and stabbed her in the back."

Japanese: これは、目撃者が“見た”ことを そのまま証言したワケですよね？
English: Now, is that testimony exactly what the witness claims to have seen?

Japanese: はあ。‥‥たしかに そう言っていたッス。
English: Yeah. ...That's what the witness told us.

Japanese: ‥‥その証言は、 大きくムジュンしています。
English: That testimony... is filled with holes.

Japanese: 被害者のコートは、まったく 汚れていないわけですからね。
English: After all, the victim's coat isn't dirty at all.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりですな。
English: Hmm... That's true.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ そいつが正解だ。
English: Ha...! It's not just true. It's the truth.

Japanese: もし、本当に決定的な 目撃証人がいたのなら‥‥
English: If there was a truly decisive witness in this case...

Japanese: あのボウヤは、最初から そいつを呼んでいたはずさ！
English: ...I'm certain that boy wonder over there would have called them in the first place!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: ...Your Honor!

Japanese: 弁護側は、その《目撃者》の 尋問を要求します！
English: The defense requests to cross-examine the eyewitness!

Japanese: その証言はアイマイで、 いきなりムジュンしていますッ！
English: The testimony presented so far is not only vague, but contradictory as well!

Japanese: どうやら、検事くん。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: その、ナゾの《目撃証人》の証言 ‥‥聞く必要がありそうですな。
English: It appears that we'll need to hear from your mystery witness after all.

Japanese: ‥‥‥フッ。 ‥‥カクゴしておくといい。
English: *sigh* You should brace yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクの事態を‥‥な。
English: ...For the brutal truth.

Japanese: 裁判長。検察側は、目撃証人を かくすつもりなど、まったくない。
English: Your Honor, the prosecution has no intention of hiding the witness from the court.

Japanese: ‥‥いつでも召喚に 応じる準備はできている。
English: We are prepared to present our witness at any time.

Japanese: わかりました。それでは さっそく、呼んでもらいましょう。
English: Very well... Please bring forth your witness at this time.

Japanese: （検事さんのヒトコト。 ちょっと、気になるな‥‥）
English: (What Mr. Edgeworth said... kind of worries me...)

Japanese: （“サイアクの事態”‥‥？）
English: (What does he mean by "the brutal truth"?)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、しめやかに 審理をつづけましょう。
English: Now, let's proceed with the testimony.

Japanese: 御剣検事くん。 よろしくおねがいします。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Please go right ahead.

Japanese: では、入廷していただこう。
English: Thank you, Your Honor. The prosecution summons...

Japanese: ‥‥あの日、吾童山中で 事件の瞬間を目撃した証人に‥‥。
English: ...the woman who saw the events that day with her very own eyes.

Japanese: （‥‥ここからが勝負よ、千尋！）
English: (This is it, Mia! The battle begins here!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 名前と職業をおねがいしよう。
English: ...Witness. What is your name and occupation?

Japanese: （な、なに？　このチンモク‥‥）
English: (Everyone is so silent that I can hear their hearts going pitter-patter...)

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥‥‥う。
English: Hmm... Ohh...

Japanese: なんかこう、アレですなあ。 あなたを見ていると、こう‥‥
English: When I look at you... How can I put it...?

Japanese: ココロが洗われるというか、 血行がサラサラになるというか、
English: You look as scrumptous as a double-double and a dozen doughnut holes...

Japanese: 思わず、軽やかに 判決を下したくなるというか。
English: I feel like I want to hurry up and hand down a verdict just to have a bite...

Japanese: （バカなコト言わないでよ！）
English: (Hey, hey! Not so fast!)

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。 前にも申し上げたが‥‥
English: ...*sigh* As I said before...

Japanese: この証人は、非常にセンサイな ココロの持ち主なのだ。
English: This witness is very sensitive and delicate.

Japanese: くれぐれも、質問には 気をつかっていただきたい。
English: I would ask the court to please exercise care when addressing her.

Japanese: くううう‥‥さすが御剣検事くん。 ジェントルマンですな。
English: Yes, indeed... Thank you, Mr. Edgeworth. You are a true gentleman.

Japanese: 弁護人！　あなたもキチンと 見習うように。
English: Ms. Fey! You could learn a lot from this man!

Japanese: （‥‥ジェントルマンなら、 私にも気をつかってほしいな）
English: (...If he's such a gentleman, he sure doesn't act like one to me.)

Japanese: あの‥‥おじさま？
English: Umm... Sir?

Japanese: ぬうううううう。 なんですかな？
English: Hmmm... Eh? Yes, my dear?

Japanese: なにぶん初めてですから、 いたらない点もあると思いますの。
English: This is my first time, so I'm sure I'll make a lot of mistakes.

Japanese: こんなムスメですが‥‥ よろしくおねがいいたしますね。
English: Anyway... I just wanted to say I'm sorry for all the trouble I might cause...

Japanese: ぬううううううううううううう。 こちらこそ、よろしくいたすッ！
English: Hmmmmm... Not at all! It's no trouble at all!

Japanese: それで‥‥ケッキョク、 名前と職業は？
English: Now then... May we please have your name and occupation?

Japanese: お名前は、あの‥‥無久井　里子 （むくいさとこ）と申しますわ。
English: My name is, umm... ...Melissa Foster.

Japanese: 大学生です。‥‥あの。 文学部の、１回生です。
English: I'm a college student... A-A freshman in the literature department.

Japanese: あのイタましい事件があったとき、 あなたは不幸にも現場にいた。
English: You were on the scene when the unfortunate event occurred, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥そして、この写真を撮影した。 まちがいありませんね？
English: ...And you were the one who took this photo? Is that accurate?

Japanese: きゃあああああっ！ な、なんてヒドい‥‥
English: Waaaaaah! H-How can you be so mean...?

Japanese: コラ！　いきなりそんなモノを 見せるヒトがありますか！
English: Now see here! What are you doing shoving that in her face like that!?

Japanese: え！　で、でも‥‥ フツウの写真じゃないですか！
English: Huh? B-But it's just a photograph! It's not like it's something dangerous!

Japanese: 今度やったら ペナルティを与えます！
English: Next time I'll be forced to penalize you!

Japanese: （‥‥ひ、ヒドいことに なってきたわ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... I don't like the turn this has taken...)

Japanese: ‥‥？ （私を‥‥見つめている‥‥？）
English: (Is she... staring at me?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。シツレイですが、 あなたさまは‥‥？
English: Umm... And you would be...?

Japanese: え。私は、弁護士ですけど。 ‥‥綾里　千尋といいます。
English: Huh? I-I'm the defense lawyer. ...My name is Mia Fey.

Japanese: そう‥‥。あなたが‥‥
English: ...I see. So you are...

Japanese: それでは、おじょうさん。 証言をおねがいできますかな？
English: Now then, young lady. Could you please give us your testimony?

Japanese: はい‥‥。 わたし、がんばりますね。
English: Yes, Your Honor... I-I'll do my best.

Japanese: ～目撃写真について～
English: -- The Witness's Photograph --

Japanese: わたし‥‥おカメラで、 お野草の撮影をしていましたの。
English: I... I was using my camera to take some pictures of wild flowers.

Japanese: 吊り橋のまん中に、２つの人影が。 なんとなく、拝見しておりました。
English: Then I noticed there were two people standing up on the suspension bridge.

Japanese: そうしたら！　いきなり 争いをお始めになられたのです！
English: Suddenly, they just started fighting!

Japanese: わたし、急いで決定的瞬間を お写真に撮りましたの。
English: That's when I hurried and took the photo that shows the crucial moment.

Japanese: それから、警察のみなさまに、 お電話をさしあげました。
English: And right after that, I called the police.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ちなみに、事件が起こったとき おじょうさんは、どこに‥‥？
English: By the way, where were you standing when the incident occurred...?

Japanese: それについては‥‥ 上面図を見ていただこう。
English: I believe the map would be of help here.

Japanese: あの。わたくしがいたのは、 このあたり‥‥です。
English: Umm... I was standing right over... here.

Japanese: 切り立ったガケにかこまれた、 春はうつくしい原っぱですのよ。
English: I was standing in a beautiful field, surrounded by tall cliffs.

Japanese: その地点から、あの写真を 撮影したわけですな。
English: So you took the photo from that location, eh.

Japanese: 検事さまに言われて、そのときの おカメラも持ってまいりました。
English: I brought the camera I was using at the time, just like Mr. Edgeworth asked me to.

Japanese: ほおほお。持ち主に似て、 かわいらしいおカメラですな。
English: Ho ho ho. What a cute camera... Just like its owner.

Japanese: 証拠品《カメラ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいしましょう。
English: ...Alright then, Ms. Fey. Time for your cross- examination.

Japanese: 証人を泣かせたら、私の 木槌がダマっていませんよ。
English: But I warn you, make the witness cry again, and you'll feel the wrath of my gavel.

Japanese: ～目撃写真について～
English: -- The Witness's Photograph --

Japanese: わたし‥‥おカメラで、 お野草の撮影をしていましたの。
English: I... I was using my camera to take some pictures of wild flowers.

Japanese: 吊り橋のまん中に、２つの人影が。 なんとなく、拝見しておりました。
English: Then I noticed there were two people standing up on the suspension bridge.

Japanese: そうしたら！　いきなり 争いをお始めになられたのです！
English: Suddenly, they just started fighting!

Japanese: わたし、急いで決定的瞬間を お写真に撮りましたの。
English: That's when I hurried and took the photo that shows the crucial moment.

Japanese: それから、警察のみなさまに、 お電話をさしあげました。
English: And right after that, I called the police.

Japanese: （尾並田さんが 犯人でない以上‥‥）
English: (If Terry Fawles isn't the criminal...)

Japanese: （あの子の証言には、どこかに おかしな部分があるはずよ！）
English: (Then there must be something strange in that girl's testimony!)

Japanese: 気をつけるんだ、コネコちゃん。
English: Be careful, Kitten.

Japanese: あの裁判長、ヒゲの先まで あの子に飼いならされてやがる。
English: That girl has the judge wrapped right around her little finger.

Japanese: ちょっとやそっとじゃ、 崩せそうにないぜ‥‥この証言。
English: You're going to have a tough time poking holes in that testimony of hers.

Japanese: （アキラカにおかしい部分を 見つけないと‥‥！）
English: (You're going to have to come up with something really good, Mia!)

Japanese: 被害者は、犯人に背を向けて、 お逃げになったのですが‥‥
English: The victim turned around and tried to run away, but...

Japanese: １０メートルほどで追いつかれて、 お背中から、お刺されに。
English: She only got about 10 yards before she was stabbed in the back.

Japanese: “お野草”‥‥ですか？
English: Did you say... "wild flowers"?

Japanese: 春の吾童山は、それは うつくしくて。おすすめですの。
English: Yes, the mountain is famous for its beautiful spring wild flowers.

Japanese: あの‥‥２月ですけど、今。
English: Umm... But it's only February.

Japanese: わたし、春まで 待ちきれなくなってしまって。
English: Well, I... I couldn't wait for Spring to come.

Japanese: ぬううむ、わかりますなあ！
English: Ho ho... I know just how you feel!

Japanese: この私なども、 ヒゲを伸ばし始めたころ‥‥
English: It's just like when I first started growing this glorious beard of mine...

Japanese: 伸びきるまで待ちきれず、 つけヒゲをしていたものです。
English: I just couldn't wait, so I wore a dyed blonde Santa beard until mine grew in properly.

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、事件のことを 話していただこうか。
English: ...Would you mind if we got back to the facts of the case, Your Honor?

Japanese: 何か、変わったところでも あったんですか？　その２人。
English: Was there anything strange about the two of them?

Japanese: わたし‥‥いけない子ですのね。
English: I... I'm a bad girl. ...I know I am.

Japanese: 吊り橋の上で、シンコクそうな お話をしているようでしたので‥‥
English: It looked like they were having a really serious conversation up there...

Japanese: つい、盗み見てしまいましたの。
English: ...So I decided to watch them. Like some kind of Peeping Tom.

Japanese: いやいやいや！ そのキモチ、よおくわかりますぞ！
English: No, not at all! Everyone is like that!

Japanese: 私も、ヒトのケンカが大好き。 もお、目がないほうでしてな。
English: I love watching other people fight, too. In fact, I can't get enough of it!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そのおかげで、 このシゴトを選んだワケです。
English: ...Actually, that's why I took this job in the first place.

Japanese: （聞いてないわよ！）
English: (Too much info, Your Honor!)

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、この２人を目にとめる のは、自然ではないだろうか。
English: ...In any case, it's perfectly natural for you to have kept watching them.

Japanese: なんと言っても、 このカッコウだからな‥‥。
English: Especially dressed as they were...

Japanese: とにかく、わたし‥‥おふたりを あたたかく見守っていましたの。
English: Well, anyway... I was watching them very closely.

Japanese: その“争い”の原因は わかりましたか？
English: Do you have any idea what they were fighting about?

Japanese: え‥‥もちろん、存じませんわ。 どうしてですの？
English: Eh? No... I have no idea. Why do you ask that?

Japanese: その２人の話している声が 聞こえたかな、と思って‥‥
English: Oh, I just thought that maybe you overheard what they said...

Japanese: わたし‥‥ヒトさまのハナシを 盗み聞きするなんて‥‥
English: I would never... I would never eavesdrop...

Japanese: そんな、はしたない子じゃ ありませんのよ！
English: I've got more class than that!

Japanese: あなたといっしょに しないように！
English: That's right, Ms. Fey! Don't drag the witness down to your level!

Japanese: （うぐぐぐ‥‥）
English: (Grrrr...!)

Japanese: どうして、写真を‥‥？
English: Why did you take a photo?

Japanese: お２人が、あまりに すごいイキオイでしたので‥‥
English: Well, the two of them were really going at it...

Japanese: わたし‥‥小さいころ、新聞記者に あこがれていたものですから‥‥
English: Ever since I was a little girl, I always wanted to be a news reporter...

Japanese: こんなとき、記者ダマシイが お目ざめになってしまうのですね。
English: I guess that part of me just kind of took over...

Japanese: （かぎりなく、ウソっぽい‥‥）
English: (Smells like a lie to me...)

Japanese: いやいや！　ありますなあ、 そういうコト。
English: Yes, I understand completely!

Japanese: 私も、いまだにムカシのユメを 捨てきれず、
English: Even now, I can't completely abandon my boyhood dreams...

Japanese: マゴを相手に、小ばなしを ヒロウすることがありますぞ！
English: I still use my grandson to test my comedy routines on!

Japanese: （裁判長、落語家がユメだったのね ‥‥どうでもいいけど）
English: (So he wanted to be a comedian, huh. ...Not that it has any bearing on this.)

Japanese: わたしにできることは、 お写真を撮ることだけでした。
English: All I could do was to use my camera.

Japanese: 決定的な瞬間を撮って、警察の みなさまに提出いたしましたの。
English: So I took the photo of the crucial moment and gave it to the police.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥事件の通報を？
English: You called the police?

Japanese: ええ。犯人のかたが、逃げて しまいそうだったものですから。
English: Yes. Because it looked to me like the murderer was going to try to escape.

Japanese: ‥‥通報が入る前から、ワレワレは すでに、動いていた。
English: ...We were already moving before the call even came in.

Japanese: 被害者のメモを発見したため、 検問の手配の最中だったのだ。
English: Thanks to the victim's note, we had already started our operation.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥。犯人にとっては 運がなかったワケですな。
English: Hmm... That was certainly tough luck for the criminal, eh.

Japanese: 被害者は‥‥ なぜ、逃げたのでしょう。
English: The victim... Why do you think she tried to run away?
Umm...

Japanese: 女性とはいえ、巡査部長です。 背中を向けて逃げるなんて‥‥
English: With her police training, she certainly knew better than to turn her back on a criminal...

Japanese: 相手は、体格のいいオトコで、 ナイフを持っていた。
English: This was a large, powerful man with a knife.

Japanese: キミが相手ならいざしらず、 フツウの女性の相手ではなかった。
English: If it had been a quaggy woman like you, I'm sure she would have acted differently.

Japanese: （“キミ”と来たか‥‥）
English: ("Quaggy"!? Why you...!)

Japanese: 私なら、たぶん川に 飛びこんでいたでしょう。
English: If it had been me, I probably would have jumped into the river!

Japanese: （何か、ひっかかるのよね‥‥）
English: (There's still something wrong with this testimony...)

Japanese: 被害者‥‥美柳　勇希さんは、 逃げきれなかった、と‥‥？
English: So you're saying Sergeant Hawthorne wasn't able to get away from him...?

Japanese: 細い吊り橋でしたし、 かなりゆれていましたから‥‥
English: Well, it's a narrow bridge and it was swaying back and forth...

Japanese: 逃げるほうも、追うほうも、 イノチがけだったと思います。
English: If you ask me, both of them were in danger of falling off.

Japanese: わたしにもっと、チカラがあれば。 お助けできたのに‥‥
English: I only wish I could have done something to help her...

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥う。 スジはとおっていますな。
English: Hmmmm... That seems to make sense.

Japanese: （そうなのかしら‥‥？ どこか、おかしいような‥‥）
English: I wonder about that... Something seems kind of off...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　その《違和感》‥‥
English: Ha...! You have a good sixth sense.

Japanese: よおく、理由を考えたほうが よさそうだぜ‥‥。
English: When you feel that something's off, that's when you need to figure out why...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: ...Witness.

Japanese: あなたの言う《決定的瞬間》とは、 これのコトですか？
English: When you said you took a photo of the crucial moment, is this what you meant?
Err...

Japanese: この写真‥‥２人の人物が 向かい合っているだけですね。
English: All I can see in this photo are two people facing each other.

Japanese: あなたは、２人の“争い”を 目撃したと証言しています。
English: You testified that you saw the two of them starting to fight.

Japanese: 私たちはふつう、それを 《決定的瞬間》と呼びます！
English: Normally that's the kind of thing we would refer to as a crucial moment!

Japanese: なぜ、その写真が 提出されていないのですか！
English: Why haven't you presented a photo like that!?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥
English: ...W-Well, you see...

Japanese: ‥‥提出された写真は、 これ１枚きり、だ‥‥。
English: ...The photo we presented was the only one there was.

Japanese: もし本当に《決定的瞬間》を 撮影するつもりだったのならば‥‥
English: But if you really wanted to capture the crucial moment...

Japanese: このあと何が起こったか？ それを写真に撮ったはずです！
English: ...then what happened next? You must have taken a photo of it!

Japanese: ぬ‥‥ぬうううううう‥‥むッ！
English: Hm... Hmmmmm... Doh!

Japanese: あの‥‥よろしいかな、 おじょうさん。
English: Err... Umm... My apologies, young lady...

Japanese: 弁護人の主張は、ある種 わからないでもありませんが‥‥？
English: But Ms. Fey's assertion is not without a certain amount of merit...

Japanese: あ、“ある種”‥‥？
English: (He can certainly downplay a situation, can't he...)

Japanese: ‥‥すみません‥‥。 わたし‥‥いけない子、ですのね。
English: ...I-I'm sorry... ...I'm a very bad girl.

Japanese: その‥‥。切らして しまったんです。‥‥フィルムを。
English: I umm... I used it all up... The film, I mean.

Japanese: フィルムを‥‥ッ！
English: You ran out of film!?

Japanese: この、お写真が‥‥ 最後の１枚だったんです。
English: Err, this photo was the last one.

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ これは、ジジツなのだ。
English: Unfortunately, that is the truth.

Japanese: ‥‥証人が事件当日に撮影した 写真は、私がすべてチェックした。
English: ...I personally examined all of the photographs she took that day.

Japanese: 他の写真は、草原でたわむれる、 証人自身の写真だった。
English: All the other photos are of the witness herself, playing among the wild flowers.

Japanese: 《証人自身》‥‥って、 それはダレが撮影したんですか？
English: The witness herself? Then who took the photos?

Japanese: あの‥‥わたしのおカメラ、 セルフタイマーがついてますから。
English: Well... You see... My camera has a timer feature built into it.

Japanese: 自分で、自分の写真を 撮影したわけですか！
English: So, you took photos of yourself!?

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥ 私にも、おぼえがありますぞお！
English: Hmm... I remember taking some photos of myself once, too!

Japanese: （そんな、不自然な‥‥）
English: (Please! No details...!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、弁護人の指摘は 決定的とは言えないようです。
English: ...It seems that Ms. Fey's assertion was not so decisive after all.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと 待ってください！
English: W-Wait! Just a minute!

Japanese: フィルムがなければ 写真は撮れませんからね。
English: Well, if she had no film left, she couldn't very well take more pictures, eh.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥ワレワレに 聞かせていただけるだろうか。
English: Ms. Foster, perhaps then you could tell us about a different sort of photo.

Japanese: あなたのココロのフィルムに 焼きついた、“事件”について。
English: Photos of the incident that you "took" with your very own eyes.

Japanese: まあ‥‥検事さま。 詩人でいらっしゃいますのね。
English: Mr. Edgeworth... You're quite the poet!

Japanese: それでは！　尋問に 戻っていただきましょう。
English: Very well then! Let's get back to the cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたの見た“争い”を ふまえて、証言してください！
English: ...Let's hear your thoughts on the fight that you witnessed!

Japanese: わかりましたわ‥‥オジサマ。
English: Yes... Mr. Judge.

Japanese: （ホンキで“今のはナシ”に されちゃったみたいね‥‥）
English: (Boy, this guy is really a sucker for sweet talk...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
どうやら‥‥法廷中が、あっちの コネコちゃんの味方、ってワケか。
English: It looks like the other kitten in the room is the one that's getting all the attention.

Japanese: ちょっぴり、セツないです。
English: Yeah, it's sickening.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、尾並田　美散に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Terry Fawles...

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
